+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-146
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_11-42-07+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_TASK_2026-05-24_11-33-44+0300.md — TASK packet driving this read-only audit
+- golden-projects/README.md — product intent (rows 5-14)
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md — per-project promise (rows 1-5)
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project — current manifest, `scenes: []` placeholder (rows 1-9)
+- golden-projects/material-zoo/.rge-project — sibling placeholder, confirms `scenes: []` is the convention
+- golden-projects/skinned-character/.rge-project — sibling placeholder
+- golden-projects/physics-puzzle/.rge-project — sibling placeholder
+- golden-projects/cad-parametric/.rge-project — sibling placeholder
+- golden-projects/stress-world/.rge-project — sibling placeholder
+- crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs — module index and `Reflect` stub note (rows 54-80)
+- crates/rge-data/src/project.rs — `Project` / `ScenePath` / `TargetTier` / `PluginRef` (rows 27-103)
+- crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs — `Scene` / `Entity` / `ComponentValue` / `Relation` (rows 33-115)
+- crates/rge-data/src/prefab.rs — `Prefab` shape (rows 27-80)
+- crates/rge-data/src/entity_ref.rs — `EntityId` ULID identity (rows 36-57)
+- crates/rge-data/src/schema_version.rs — `SchemaVersion` wire format (rows 60-71)
+- crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs — canonical pretty-print + fixture builders (rows 19-158)
+- crates/rge-data/tests/round_trip.rs — round-trip + canonical-builder gates (rows 27-157)
+- crates/rge-data/tests/schema_validation.rs — fixture parse + identity gates (rows 129-177)
+- crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/sample_project.rge-project — canonical project on-disk shape
+- crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/sample_scene.rge-scene — canonical scene on-disk shape
+- crates/editor-shell/tests/pie_round_trip.rs — programmatic scene; not a `.rge-scene` loader
+- editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs — glTF/CAD entry; does not load `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene`
+- crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs — procedurally-built 1000-cube perf gate; not a golden-projects consumer
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_TASK_2026-05-24_11-33-44+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This dispatch is a read-only audit.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+No source / test / Cargo / workflow / script / doctrine / status / golden-project edits. The only new artifact is this EXEC report (plus its sidecar after finalization).
+
+## 5-Question Golden Simple-Scene Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. What exists today under golden-projects/simple-scene?
+
+The directory holds exactly two files and **no scene file**:
+
+- `golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md` (5 lines total): line 3 states the
+  per-project promise — *"Basic load + transform + camera + light render"* —
+  and line 5 cites it as "CI regression harness" per `PLAN.md` §13 + `WAVES.md`
+  W21.
+- `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` (9 lines total): RON
+  `Project(...)` literal with `version: "0.1.0"` (line 2), `name:
+  "simple-scene"` (line 3), `description: "Basic load + transform + camera +
+  light render"` (line 4), `target_tiers: ["desktop"]` (line 5), `plugins: []`
+  (line 6), **`scenes: [],`** (line 7), and `schema_version: "0.1.0"` (line 8).
+
+The `scenes: []` empty list is a placeholder convention shared across **every**
+golden project — the other five (`material-zoo`, `skinned-character`,
+`physics-puzzle`, `cad-parametric`, `stress-world`) all carry the same shape
+with `scenes: []` and `plugins: []`. A `Glob "golden-projects/**/*"` returns
+exactly the 6 `.rge-project` manifests, 6 `README.md` files, and the top-level
+`README.md` — there are **no `.rge-scene` files, no `scenes/` subdirectories,
+no fixtures, and no generated assets** anywhere under `golden-projects/`.
+
+The product intent — `golden-projects/README.md` rows 5-7 — promises that the
+suite should be exercised through CI on every major change, with exit
+criteria (row 14): *"all 6 load, run 60 ticks, screenshot match within
+tolerance, byte-identical cook output"*. That intent is documentation only;
+see Q3 for whether any current consumer exists.
+
+A schema mismatch worth flagging: the on-disk manifest carries
+`schema_version: "0.1.0"` as a **separate field** (line 8), but the current
+`Project` struct in `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` (rows 27-44) declares no
+such field — only `version: SchemaVersion`. This is consistent across all six
+golden manifests, so the divergence is a placeholder-vs-schema observation
+rather than simple-scene-specific. Surfaced as a finding for the reviewer;
+not in scope to fix.
+
+### Q2. What is the current rge-data project and scene schema surface?
+
+The `rge-data` crate is the RON serde+migration layer (see
+`crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs` rows 1-50 module docstring; "stateless serde +
+migration layer", no PIE state). The container shapes a scaffold must obey:
+
+- **`Project`** (`crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows 27-44): fields
+  `version: SchemaVersion`, `name: String`, `description: String`,
+  `target_tiers: Vec<TargetTier>`, `plugins: Vec<PluginRef>`,
+  `scenes: Vec<ScenePath>`. No additional `schema_version` field.
+- **`ScenePath`** (`crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows 89-103):
+  `#[serde(transparent)] pub struct ScenePath(pub String);` — on-disk RON is
+  the bare string `"scenes/foo.rge-scene"`. Convention: project-root-relative
+  path. Doc comment (rows 42-43): *"The editor loads the first entry as the
+  start scene unless overridden by user prefs."*
+- **`TargetTier`** (`crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows 63-74):
+  `#[serde(rename_all = "lowercase")]` enum `Desktop | Mobile | Web |
+  Headless` — RON wire form `desktop`/`headless`/etc.
+- **`PluginRef`** (`crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows 79-87): `id:
+  String`, `version_req: String`.
+- **`Scene`** (`crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` rows 33-45): `version:
+  SchemaVersion`, `name: String`, `entities: Vec<Entity>`, `root_entities:
+  Vec<EntityId>` — IDs of entities with no `ChildOf` parent.
+- **`Entity`** (`crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` rows 68-81): `id: EntityId`,
+  `name: String`, `components: Vec<ComponentValue>`, `relations:
+  Vec<Relation>`.
+- **`ComponentValue`** (`crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` rows 89-95):
+  reflection-neutral envelope of `type_id: String` (e.g.
+  `"rge::components::Transform"`) and `data: String` (a RON literal). Two-
+  phase deserialize: the scene parses first, then a downstream caller would
+  walk `data` through reflection-aware bridging — see lib.rs rows 36-39 and
+  the **`Reflect` stub** note (lib.rs rows 70-80): *"This crate never
+  invokes any methods on a `Reflect` impl — the trait only exists so
+  downstream calling code can refer to a stable name when staging
+  integration. Once W02 lands, replace this module with `pub use
+  rge_kernel_types::Reflect;`."* This is load-bearing for Q3/Q4 — the
+  `type_id` strings in the on-disk fixtures are placeholder paths, not
+  references to registered runtime components.
+- **`Relation`** (`crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` rows 99-115): enum
+  `ChildOf { parent: EntityId }` | `LinkedTo { kind: String, target:
+  EntityId }`.
+- **`EntityId`** (`crates/rge-data/src/entity_ref.rs` rows 36-57):
+  `#[serde(transparent)]` ULID; serializes as the 26-char Crockford base32
+  string (see `tests/fixtures/sample_scene.rge-scene` rows 6, 17, 27 for the
+  on-disk form `"0000000000000G000000000000"`). `Display` is the lossy
+  `e_<8 hex>` form for diagnostics only.
+- **`SchemaVersion`** (`crates/rge-data/src/schema_version.rs` rows 60-71):
+  `(major: u8, minor: u8, patch: u8)`; wire form is a quoted dotted-triple
+  string `"0.1.0"` per the visitor in this module.
+- **Migration registry**
+  (`crates/rge-data/src/migration.rs` rows 53-100,
+  `crates/rge-data/tests/schema_validation.rs` rows 153-177): each
+  `FileKind` (`Project` / `Scene` / `Prefab`) carries a v0.0 → v0.1 chain;
+  current schema target is `SchemaVersion::V0_1_0`.
+
+The **canonical pretty-print configuration** that defines byte-identical
+RON output is duplicated in two places that the W14 wave deliberately keeps
+in lockstep: `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs` rows 19-27 and
+`crates/rge-data/tests/round_trip.rs` rows 27-35 — `depth_limit(64)`,
+`new_line("\n")`, `indentor("    ")`, `struct_names(true)`,
+`separate_tuple_members(false)`, `enumerate_arrays(false)`, plus a trailing
+newline appended in `ser` (rows 31-34 / rows 37-43). Any future scaffold
+that needs byte-identical round-trip MUST emit through this same config.
+
+The **canonical fixture-driven contract** for a scene is encoded as: two
+entities (one `Camera` with a `Transform` `ComponentValue`, one
+`ChildLight` with `ChildOf` relation to the camera and its own `Transform`),
+`root_entities: [parent]` — see `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs`
+rows 62-95 and the on-disk `crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/sample_scene
+.rge-scene` rows 1-35. The fixture's `Transform` data is a string-encoded
+RON literal `"(translation:(...),rotation:(...),scale:(...))"` but is **never
+parsed into a typed Transform inside this crate**; nothing downstream of
+`rge-data` consumes the `data:` payload today (see Q3).
+
+### Q3. Which runtime or editor consumers would actually exercise a golden simple-scene today?
+
+A repository-wide grep for `rge_data` / `rge-data` /
+`golden-projects` / `simple-scene` returned the following consumer surfaces
+(grep results in the command log below):
+
+| # | Candidate consumer | Loads `golden-projects/simple-scene/...`? | Loads any `.rge-project` / `.rge-scene`? | Classification |
+|---|---|---|---|---|
+| 1 | `crates/rge-data/tests/round_trip.rs` (rows 20-22, 129-186) | No | Yes — `include_str!("fixtures/sample_*.rge-{project,scene,prefab}")` only | `test-only-consumer` |
+| 2 | `crates/rge-data/tests/schema_validation.rs` (rows 8-10, 129-151) | No | Yes — same in-crate fixtures only | `test-only-consumer` |
+| 3 | `crates/rge-data/tests/migration_test.rs` | No | Yes — `tests/fixtures/v0.0_to_v0.1_migration_input.rge-scene` only | `test-only-consumer` |
+| 4 | `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs` (rows 137-156) | No | No — writes RON only into `crates/rge-data/tests/fixtures/` | `test-only-consumer` |
+| 5 | `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` (rows 1-80) | No | No — entry path is CAD `CuboidOp` or `--glb <path>` via `import_glb`; no `rge_data` import (verified by grep — `rge_data` does not appear in `editor/**`) | `future-consumer` |
+| 6 | `crates/editor-shell/**` (`pie_round_trip.rs` rows 9-12, 44-62; `tests/fixtures/scene_100_entities.ron` rows 1-19) | No | No — scenes are built programmatically via `EditorShell::new()` + `world_mut().spawn()`; the `.ron` fixture comment (rows 7-9) explicitly says *"the loader is a tiny RON parser inside the round-trip test so we don't tie the test to a not-yet-shipped scene-loader crate"* | `future-consumer` |
+| 7 | `crates/gfx/tests/gate_a_simple_scene_60fps.rs` (rows 33-156) and `gate_a_simple_scene_depth_60fps.rs` | No | No — names refer to PLAN.md §6.3 Gate A; 1000 cubes are pushed in code via `push_cube(...)`, vertices/indices/material built procedurally; the file does not import `rge_data` | `future-consumer` |
+| 8 | `.github/**` workflows | No (grep returned `No files found` for `golden-projects` / `simple-scene` under `.github`) | No | `future-consumer` |
+| 9 | `tools/ci/**` (referenced by `golden-projects/README.md` line 16) | n/a — `Glob "tools/ci/**/*"` returns `No files found` | n/a | `future-consumer` |
+| 10 | Project/scene loader API on `rge-data` (parse → migrate chain → typed struct) | n/a | Test-level only | `consumer-present` (for the rge-data crate's own in-crate fixtures), `future-consumer` (for any disk path outside the crate's own fixtures) |
+| 11 | Render-frame comparison / screenshot-baseline / byte-identical-cook harness implied by `golden-projects/README.md` line 14 | No | No — no such harness exists anywhere in the repo (no screenshot baseline, no cook output comparator, no image-diff tool) | `needs-design` |
+| 12 | Runtime that turns a `ComponentValue { type_id: "rge::components::Transform", ... }` into a real ECS `Transform` component on a `kernel/ecs::World` | n/a | n/a — bridging requires `kernel/types::Reflect` (W02), which `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs` rows 70-80 explicitly mark as **not yet landed**; the `Reflect` trait is a local marker stub | `needs-design` |
+
+Summary: **no current consumer exercises `golden-projects/simple-scene/`
+specifically**. The `rge-data` crate's tests exercise the schema only
+through its own in-crate `tests/fixtures/`, never reading from the
+`golden-projects/` directory. The gfx crate's `gate_a_simple_scene_*`
+tests share the descriptive name "simple-scene" with PLAN.md §6.3 Gate A
+but are procedural perf gates that never touch `.rge-scene` files or
+`rge-data`. The editor binary loads CAD ops or glTF, not `.rge-project` /
+`.rge-scene`.
+
+### Q4. What is the smallest safe follow-up if the scaffold is bounded?
+
+The scaffold is **not** bounded by current code. Two facts make Q4 a
+policy question, not an implementation:
+
+1. **The transform/camera/light/render half of the README promise has no
+   current consumer.** Per Q3 rows 11-12 and the explicit Halt Condition in
+   the TASK packet (*"Do not propose generated binary assets, screenshot
+   baselines, or a renderer comparison harness unless Q3 shows an existing
+   consumer already exists in code"*), a scaffold that authors a
+   `.rge-scene` with `Transform` / `Camera` / `Light` components would
+   either (a) invent component type IDs and renderer expectations the
+   codebase doesn't honor today, or (b) require a new render-frame /
+   screenshot-baseline consumer to give those components meaning. Both are
+   excluded by the audit's halt conditions.
+
+2. **A pure schema-load smoke is technically possible** (parse the
+   manifest's `scenes: [...]` list via `rge_data::Project`, parse each
+   referenced `.rge-scene` via `rge_data::Scene`, assert round-trip is
+   byte-identical via the existing `pretty_config`). But this would
+   downscope the README's stated promise from *"basic load, transform,
+   camera, and light render"* to *"basic schema load only"* — and that
+   downscope is a **golden-project policy decision**, not a mechanical
+   scaffold. It also requires choosing where the smoke test lives (under
+   `crates/rge-data/tests/`? under a new `tests/golden-projects/`
+   harness? under `tools/ci/`?), which is itself a layout decision the
+   audit has no authority to make.
+
+Per the TASK packet's instruction (*"If the next step is a design or
+golden-project policy choice, Q4 states the decision required instead of
+inventing files"*), Q4 names the decisions, not files.
+
+**Decisions required from the human arbiter** before task #32 can be
+bounded:
+
+- **D1 — Scope.** Does `simple-scene/` ship as (a) schema-load smoke only
+  (parse `.rge-project` + at-most-one minimal `.rge-scene`, assert
+  round-trip), (b) load + tick (requires a typed Transform component, a
+  scene-loader → ECS bridge, and a place to register components — none
+  exists today), or (c) full load + transform + camera + light render
+  with a screenshot/byte-cook comparison (requires a renderer-comparison
+  harness, image-diff tooling, and a cooked-output baseline — none of
+  which exist)? Today only path (a) is implementable without inventing
+  schema, types, or consumers.
+- **D2 — Component-type identity.** If a `.rge-scene` is authored under
+  `golden-projects/simple-scene/`, what `type_id` strings does it carry?
+  The current fixtures use placeholder paths like
+  `"rge::components::Transform"` (see
+  `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs` rows 74-77), but no
+  `rge::components::Transform` is registered anywhere in the workspace,
+  and the `Reflect` bridge is W02-deferred (see
+  `crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs` rows 70-80). Choosing a `type_id` is
+  effectively naming a not-yet-existing component contract.
+- **D3 — Schema-vs-manifest divergence.** The current
+  `golden-projects/*/.rge-project` files all carry a `schema_version:
+  "0.1.0"` field that the `Project` struct in
+  `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` does not declare. Should task #32 (i)
+  align all six manifests to the current `Project` schema, (ii) extend
+  the `Project` schema to honor `schema_version`, or (iii) leave both as
+  is? This is an `rge-data` / golden-project policy choice.
+- **D4 — Test/CI placement.** Where does the golden-projects load smoke
+  live — a new `tests/golden_projects.rs` integration test under
+  `crates/rge-data/`, a dedicated `tools/ci/` script (today
+  `Glob "tools/ci/**/*"` returns no files), or a new
+  `golden-projects/` workspace member? The `golden-projects/README.md`
+  line 16 references *"dedicated CI workflows in `tools/ci/`"* that do
+  not yet exist on disk.
+
+Because each of D1-D4 is a golden-project policy / design choice (not an
+already-implied bounded scaffold), Q4 does **not** name allowed files,
+tests, gates, MUST NOT list, or halt conditions — per the TASK packet
+that block is reserved for the case where the current schema and current
+consumer pressure already make a scaffold bounded.
+
+### Q5. What should task #32 be?
+
+**NEEDS_HUMAN.**
+
+Rationale: the smallest safe follow-up requires picking a golden-project
+scope policy (Q4 D1) before any executor can write a bounded task. The
+current schema can express only a load-only scene; the README's "camera +
+light render" promise requires either (a) a downscope decision or (b) a
+broader consumer-first design (renderer comparison harness, image baseline
+system, cook output comparator) — and Q3 rows 11-12 show no such consumer
+exists in code today, which the TASK's halt conditions and acceptance
+criteria forbid the audit from inventing. The human arbiter must answer
+D1-D4 before task #32 can be scoped.
+
+## Command Log
+
+Read-only file searches and inspections used to support Q1-Q5 (sequence
+matches execution order; intermediate file `Read` calls are batched into
+the per-section evidence above):
+
+- `Read ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_TASK_2026-05-24_11-33-44+0300.md` — the TASK
+  packet driving this audit (rows 1-149).
+- `Bash: git status --short --untracked-files=no` — pre-audit; empty
+  output (no tracked changes).
+- `Read ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md` — protocol confirmation,
+  packet shape, footer format (rows 1-448).
+- `Glob golden-projects/**/*` → 13 entries: 6 `.rge-project` manifests, 6
+  per-project `README.md`, 1 top-level `README.md`. **Confirms absence of
+  any `.rge-scene` file anywhere under `golden-projects/`.**
+- `Read golden-projects/README.md` (full, 17 rows) — product intent.
+- `Read golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md` (full, 5 rows).
+- `Read golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` (full, 9 rows) — the
+  empty `scenes: []` is row 7; the `schema_version: "0.1.0"` divergence is
+  row 8.
+- `Read` on each of the five sibling `.rge-project` manifests
+  (`material-zoo`, `skinned-character`, `physics-puzzle`, `cad-parametric`,
+  `stress-world`) — confirms shared placeholder convention.
+- `Glob crates/rge-data/**/*` → 18 entries: 9 `src/*.rs`, 4
+  `tests/fixtures/*`, 3 `tests/*.rs`, 1 `examples/bake_fixtures.rs`, 1
+  `Cargo.toml`.
+- `Read crates/rge-data/src/lib.rs` (full, 80 rows) — module index and
+  `Reflect` stub note (rows 70-80, load-bearing for Q3/Q4).
+- `Read crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` (full, 147 rows) — `Project`,
+  `TargetTier`, `PluginRef`, `ScenePath` definitions.
+- `Read crates/rge-data/src/scene.rs` (full, 172 rows) — `Scene`,
+  `Entity`, `ComponentValue`, `Relation` definitions and test fixture

+Human Arbiter. The following are observations the next role may want to

+  `golden-projects/*/.rge-project` files carry a `schema_version: "0.1.0"`
+  field that `crates/rge-data/src/project.rs` rows 27-44 does not declare.
+  RON is tolerant on extra fields with serde's default, but this fixture

+  16 promises *"dedicated CI workflows in `tools/ci/`"*; `Glob

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-146
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-24_11-33-44+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- .ai/dispatch.tasks.md
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md
+- ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md
+- golden-projects/README.md
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md
+- golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project
+- golden-projects/*/.rge-project
+- crates/rge-data/src/**
+- crates/rge-data/tests/**
+- crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs
+- editor/**
+- crates/editor-shell/**
+- Status.md
+- HANDOFF.md
+- change.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+- GitHub issue #146
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Perform GitHub issue #146 as a read-only golden simple-scene preflight. The golden-project suite is product-facing regression infrastructure: `golden-projects/README.md` says `simple-scene/` should cover basic load, transform, camera, and light render. Today `golden-projects/simple-scene/` contains a README and `.rge-project`, but its manifest has an empty `scenes: []` list and no scene file under that project. This audit must inspect the current `golden-projects/simple-scene` state, the `rge-data` project/scene schema, and any current loader/test/editor consumer surfaces so task #32 is precise instead of inventing an unsupported golden-project format.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- Any file under `golden-projects/**`, including `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`, `golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md`, scene files, fixtures, generated assets, or golden-project documentation.
+- Any `crates/**` source, test, example, fixture, schema, generated asset, or data file.
+- Any `editor/**` file.
+- Any `kernel/**` file.
+- Any `.github/**` workflow file.
+- Any Cargo file, including `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workspace manifests, generated Cargo metadata, or dependency declarations.
+- Any PowerShell script, shell script, schema, architecture lint, doctrine document, status document, planning document, ADR, architecture document, or root-level repository document.
+- Any existing handoff packet or existing dispatch log, including this TASK packet after execution begins.
+- Any GitHub issue, label, PR, branch, review, comment, commit, remote state, or queue metadata except the queue runner's normal issue lifecycle for this dispatch.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar only if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it while finalizing the EXEC report.
+- This dispatch's own queue-runner log under `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` only if the queue runner creates it as part of normal dispatch operation.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New scene files, golden-project files, generated binary assets, screenshot baselines, renderer comparison fixtures, source files, test files, example files, docs, architecture docs, Cargo files, dependency files, schemas, scripts, workflows, architecture lints, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CLOSEOUT packets, issue notes, or design documents.
+- Any handoff packet other than this dispatch's single `ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.md` report and optional matching `.meta.json` sidecar.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One read-only `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet for ISSUE-146.
+- The EXEC report contains a literal `## 5-Question Golden Simple-Scene Preflight Answer Block` section.
+- Under that section, the EXEC report contains these exact Q1-Q5 heading lines, as Markdown headings:
+  - `### Q1. What exists today under golden-projects/simple-scene?`
+  - `### Q2. What is the current rge-data project and scene schema surface?`
+  - `### Q3. Which runtime or editor consumers would actually exercise a golden simple-scene today?`
+  - `### Q4. What is the smallest safe follow-up if the scaffold is bounded?`
+  - `### Q5. What should task #32 be?`
+- Q1 states the current `golden-projects/simple-scene` manifest, README, scene-list, and missing-file state with file/line evidence.
+- Q2 summarizes the concrete `rge-data` `Project`, `Scene`, entity, component, relation, parse/serialize, and fixture conventions that a simple-scene scaffold must obey, with file/line evidence.
+- Q3 identifies whether a load-only fixture, render-frame smoke, bake-fixture path, or no current consumer exists. Each candidate consumer must be classified as exactly one of `consumer-present`, `test-only-consumer`, `future-consumer`, or `needs-design`.
+- Q4 names exact allowed files, test additions, focused gates, canonical gates, MUST NOT list, and halt conditions only if the current schema and current consumer pressure already make a scaffold bounded. If the next step is a design or golden-project policy choice, Q4 states the decision required instead of inventing files.
+- Q5 recommends exactly one of: a bounded scaffold implementation task, a narrower read-only audit, a docs-only clarification, or `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The EXEC report includes a concise command log for the read-only file searches and inspections used to support Q1-Q5.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- The dispatch is read-only except for its own generated EXEC report, optional matching sidecar, and possible queue-runner log.
+- The EXEC report includes the exact five-question block title and exact Q1-Q5 heading lines listed in Deliverables.
+- Q1 cites file/line evidence for the current `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project` state, including the empty `scenes: []` list, and for the README's stated purpose.
+- Q2 cites file/line evidence for the current `rge-data` project/scene schema surface, including project manifest shape, scene shape, entity/component/relation conventions, and parse/serialize or fixture conventions.
+- Q3 classifies every plausible current or candidate consumer using only these four classifications: `consumer-present`, `test-only-consumer`, `future-consumer`, or `needs-design`.
+- The EXEC report must not propose generated binary assets, screenshot baselines, or a renderer comparison harness unless Q3 shows a current consumer already exists in code and cites that consumer with file/line evidence.
+- If the next step requires choosing a golden-project policy rather than applying an already-bounded scaffold, Q5 must end with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; the EXEC report must use `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, include `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, and end with footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+- If a bounded follow-up exists, Q5 must name one task #32 route with exact allowed files, exact tests, exact MUST NOT list, focused gates, canonical gates, and halt conditions. The EXEC report then uses normal successful EXEC routing to the reviewer.
+- The final worktree check confirms no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, golden-project, existing-packet, issue-label, or other forbidden edit occurred.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- Do not implement the simple-scene scaffold in this dispatch.
+- Do not add or edit `.rge-project` manifests, scene files, fixtures, generated assets, screenshot baselines, binary assets, renderer comparison harnesses, tests, examples, source, Cargo files, workflows, scripts, docs, doctrine, or status files.
+- Do not infer a scene-file format from the golden-project directory listing alone; answer from the current `rge-data` schema and current consumers.
+- Do not treat `golden-projects/README.md` product intent as proof that a render comparison consumer exists today. Q3 must cite current code if a current consumer is claimed.
+- Do not propose a renderer comparison harness, image baseline system, or generated asset pipeline unless Q3 proves an existing consumer already exercises that path.
+- Do not choose a golden-project policy for the project. If the current schema supports only a load-only scaffold while the README promise requires camera/light/render policy, route to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- Do not infer dispatch completion from missing `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packets; this orchestrator dispatch normally writes TASK and EXEC packets only.
+- Do not commit, push, open a PR, create or edit GitHub issues, change labels, or write follow-up packets.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before any audit work. Document all pre-existing tracked changes if any are visible. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated user changes.
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` after writing the EXEC report. Confirm that visible tracked changes, if any, are limited to this dispatch's own `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-146_*` packet artifacts and/or this dispatch's own `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact, and that no tracked source, test, Cargo, workflow, script, doctrine, status-doc, golden-project, existing-packet, issue-label, or other forbidden file changed.
+
+No cargo, formatter, architecture-lint, renderer, screenshot, or test command is required as an Executor gate for this read-only audit. If the orchestrator or queue runner runs repository verification after EXEC and that verification fails outside this audit's allowed artifact surface, do not fix source or tests; halt/report according to the halt conditions below.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without broadening scope and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source, tests, Cargo, workflows, scripts, doctrine, status docs, golden-project files, issues, labels, existing packets, or any other forbidden file.
+- The current code cannot be inspected enough to provide file/line evidence for the current simple-scene manifest state and the current `rge-data` project/scene schema surface.
+- The answer requires inventing new `rge-data` schema, new component type IDs, new renderer expectations, a new golden-project runner, or a new golden-project policy before a human chooses the policy.
+- The smallest follow-up would need generated binary assets, screenshot baselines, or a real renderer comparison harness without Q3 showing a current consumer already exists.
+- The current schema can express only a load-only scene and the README's camera/light/render promise needs a broader golden-project policy decision. Complete the five-question block and route to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The audit finds no current consumer that can exercise a golden simple-scene and no bounded scaffold policy is already implied by current code. Complete the five-question block and route to `NEEDS_HUMAN` rather than inventing work.
+- Any required verification or repository-state check shows tracked changes outside this dispatch's own allowed handoff/log artifacts.
+- Any forbidden edit or forbidden artifact is created. Stop immediately, do not expand the diff, and report the deviation in the EXEC report.
+- A correction prompt asks the Executor to fix any source/test/Cargo/workflow/script/doc/status/golden-project failure for this read-only audit. Do not execute that correction; write or update the allowed EXEC report with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `EXEC_STATUS: blocked`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`, and `EXIT_CODE: 0`.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+This dispatch comes directly from `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` task #31 and GitHub issue #146. It is a golden-projects simple-scene audit, not a scaffold implementation. The audit should inspect `golden-projects/README.md`, `golden-projects/simple-scene/README.md`, `golden-projects/simple-scene/.rge-project`, other golden project manifests only for placeholder conventions, `crates/rge-data/src/**`, `crates/rge-data/tests/**`, `crates/rge-data/examples/bake_fixtures.rs`, and editor or shell project/scene loading code only as needed to identify real consumers. Use `Status.md`, `HANDOFF.md`, and `change.md` as historical evidence only; do not edit them.
+
+The central discipline is evidence before scaffold. The current `simple-scene` manifest is known to be empty, but task #32 should not be invented from product intent alone. If current code supports a bounded load-only `.rge-scene` scaffold and has a current load/test consumer, name that narrow route. If camera/light/render acceptance requires choosing policy or building a new consumer first, route to `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-146_EXEC_*.md` report, plus its optional generated sidecar and the queue runner's own log if applicable.
+
+Verbatim review-gate strings from the issue body:
+
+```text

+MUST produce a 5-question Golden Simple-Scene Preflight Answer Block with Q1-Q5 headings exactly as specified in the brief
+MUST cite file/line evidence for the current simple-scene manifest state and the rge-data project/scene schema surface
+MUST classify candidate consumers as consumer-present, test-only-consumer, future-consumer, or needs-design
+MUST NOT propose generated binary assets, screenshot baselines, or a renderer comparison harness unless Q3 shows a current consumer already exists
+MUST end with NEEDS_HUMAN if the next step requires choosing a golden-project policy rather than applying an already-bounded scaffold
+MUST run git status --short --untracked-files=no before and after EXEC and confirm only this dispatch's own ai_handoffs/log artifacts changed
+```
+
